span.p:
Have you PROVEN a CPU/cache
bottleneck from iterating many
entities? (profiled, not assumed)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
DEFAULT TO OOP / ENGINE-NATIVE
GameObject/Actor + components;
simpler, matches the engine idiom,
fastest to iterate — most games never need ECS

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is the hot path iterating
thousands+ of homogeneous
entities each frame?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
TARGETED OPTIMIZATION FIRST
jobify / pool / batch the specific hot path
before re-architecting the whole entity model

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Does the engine offer a
first-class ECS/data-oriented path,
and does the team know it?
